Links._comment: PublicBaseUrl is the redirect site's base (e.g. https://shrtlynx.com), used to build the full short URL a QR code encodes. Empty = bare code. A link pinned to a verified custom domain uses that domain instead.

Cors._comment: Exact frontend origins allowed to call the API cross-origin (credentials enabled). Empty = same-origin only.

Jwt._comment: SigningKey is a secret — set a 32+ char value via user-secrets/env (Jwt:SigningKey). The app fails to start on the placeholder. SameSite=None (with Secure) for cross-origin frontends.

Email._comment: Mode: Resend (send all via Resend) | Log (dev: log every email) | Hybrid (Resend for DeliverableDomains, log the rest). Set Mode=Hybrid + DeliverableDomains in appsettings.Development.json/user-secrets to grab magic links for non-deliverable test addresses from the console.

Resend._comment: ApiKey comes from user-secrets (Resend:ApiKey). FromAddress must be a Resend-verified domain in prod; onboarding@resend.dev delivers only to your own account email for local testing.

VisitSink._comment: IpHashPepper is a secret — set it per environment via user-secrets/env (VisitSink:IpHashPepper). Empty = unkeyed hashing (dev only).

Threads._comment: The Threads-specific app ID/secret from developers.facebook.com (App Settings → Basic → 'Threads app ID'/'Threads app secret' — a separate credential pair from the app-level App ID/Secret shown above them on the same page; a future Facebook/Instagram connector would get its own section here instead of reusing this one). AppSecret comes from user-secrets/Railway env (Threads:AppSecret) — never commit it. The OAuth authorize/callback endpoints live on Admin (shortlynx.dev); Core only needs these to sign publish/metrics calls with appsecret_proof.
